cv_text:
## Mina Vaez

<EMAIL> | <PHONE> | linkedin.com/in/mina-vaez | <LOCATION>, <LOCATION>

## R&D Senior Scientist

PhD-trained Biomedical Scientist with 5+ years of hands-on experience in biomaterials, polymer engineering, and medical device research and development. Proven track record in collagen and biopolymer-based material design , mechanical and biochemical characterization , and GMP/GLP compliance. Experienced in cross-functional collaboration , regulatory documentation, and project leadership from concept through validation.

## EDUCATION

Ph.D. in Biomedical Engineering, Faculty of Dentistry, University of Toronto, <LOCATION> • 2025

M.Sc. in Biomaterial Engineering, University of Tehran, <LOCATION> •

2019

B.Sc. in Material Science and Engineering, Iran University of Science and Technology, <LOCATION> • 2016

## SKILLS

Biomaterials & Polymer Science: Collagen scaffolds, gelatin, CMC, hydrogels, electrospinning, material modification, degradation studies, and sterilization methods.

Characterization Techniques: FTIR, SEM, AFM, XRD, viscometry, HPLC, mechanical testing, spectroscopy, and protein-ligand conjugation.

Regulatory & Quality Systems: GMP/GLP, ISO 10993 familiarity, SOPs, risk assessment, documentation for Class I/II devices.

Biological & Biocompatibility Testing: Cytotoxicity assays, ELISA, enzymatic activity, in vitro and ex vivo models.

Project Management & Communication: Technical reporting, cross-team collaboration, mentoring, data presentation, and scientific publications.

Tools & Software: GraphPad Prism, Origin, MS Office, ImageJ, SPSS, ChemDraw.

## EXPERIENCE

## Postdoctoral Fellow, Faculty of Dentistry, University of Toronto · 09/2025-Present

- Conducting advanced research on the application of artificial intelligence and machine learning models (CNNs) for microscopic and clinical images for automated feature extraction and pattern recognition to enhance diagnostic precision .
- Designing and executing mechanical and biochemical characterization studies of material-tissue interaction.

## Graduate Researcher, Bozec-lab, Faculty of Dentistry, University of Toronto · <PHONE>

- Developed and optimized collagen-based scaffolds and biomimetic materials with enhanced mechanical stability and biocompatibility.
- Conducted FTIR, AFM, and mechanical testing to correlate material structure with biological performance.
- Managed biocompatibility and degradation studies, ensuring adherence to biosafety and GMP standards.
- Collaborated with interdisciplinary teams on therapeutic material design, ensuring compliance with biosafety and discovery quality standards.
- Mentored junior researchers in experimental design, documentation, and adherence to GLP and GMP standards.
- Led preparation of manuscripts, abstracts, and presentations, resulting in 8+ peer-reviewed publications and 5+ international conference presentations. (Google Scholar)

## Regulatory compliance manager in Bonyan Darman Parsian Company, <LOCATION> · <PHONE>

- Oversaw regulatory submissions and compliance for Class I and II medical devices under GMP frameworks.
- Collaborated  with  national  regulatory  authorities  and  ensured  technical  documentation  met  ISO  13485 requirements.

## Regulatory and Quality Specialist in <CANDIDATE_NAME> Parsian Company, <LOCATION> · <PHONE>

- Assisted in preparing regulatory submissions and technical documentation for medical devices.
- Tracked changes in national regulatory requirements and provided summaries to senior management.

## Graduate Researcher, University of Tehran · <PHONE>

- Developed core-shell electrospun NO releasing wound dressing for enhanced wound-healing.
- Presented research findings in nationally recognized conferences.
- Published paper in peer-reviewed journals through inter-institutional collaborations.

## HONORS & AWARDS

- University of Toronto Student Leadership Award (UTSLA) · 2024
- Travel award for CBS conference sponsored by Humabiologics Inc. · 2022
- Wilson G. Harron 3 Minute Thesis Competition Award · 2021
- Dr. <CANDIDATE_NAME> · 2020 & 2021
- Faculty of Dentistry Harron scholarship · 2021-2023

## VOLUNTEERING & LEADERSHIP

- President, Graduate and Post-Graduate Dental Students Society (GPDSS) · 2023-2024
- VP Learning and Development, Graduate Super Women Engineers (GradSWE U of T) · 2023-2024
- Social Director , UofT Biomedical Engineering Students' Association (BESA) · 2022-2023
- Treasurer , Graduate and Post-Graduate Dental Students Society (GPDSS) · 2022-2023
- Publication Co-Chair , Toronto Biomedical Engineering Conference · 2022

## TRAINING & CERTIFICATES

- Biomanufacturing for Advanced Therapies - Quality Control and Microbiology (CATTI & OBIO, University of Guelph) · 2025
- Good Clinical Practice (Novartis) · 2024
- Cosmetic Science and Ingredients Specialization (Olay) · 2025
- Business Fundamentals (mini-MBA) (by Graduate Management Consulting Association) · 2024
- Commercialization of regenerative medicine (by medicine by design) · 2023
- Atomic Force Microscopy trainer and mentor, CAMiLoD, University of Toronto ·2021
- Certified technical officer of Medical Equipment Manufacturers by Iranian Medical Device Society · 2017

## PATENT

<CANDIDATE_NAME>, <CANDIDATE_NAME>,  <CANDIDATE_NAME>,  ' Generating  Electricity  by  Foot  Pressure  throughout Walking ', February 2010, Department of Companies Registration & Industrial Property. (IR-63685)
pitch_text:
## Document 2: Assignment 4 (Pitch)

Selected Job Offer Position: Senior Scientist, R&D (Personal Care / Biomaterials) Target: Deciem (The Abnormal Beauty Company) or Johnson & Johnson Key Requirements:

- PhD in Chemistry, Bioengineering, or related field.
- Experience with biopolymers, proteins (collagen), and formulations.
- Knowledge of GLP/GMP and regulatory standards.
- Ability to lead projects from concept to validation.

The Elevator Pitch

"Hi, I'm <CANDIDATE_NAME>. I am a Biomedical Scientist who bridges the gap between molecular innovation and regulatory compliance.

I have a PhD in Biomedical Engineering where I specialized in collagen-based scaffolds and hydrogels, giving me deep expertise in the very ingredients that drive high-performance skincare and wound care. But unlike many researchers, I also have professional experience as a Regulatory Compliance Manager. I've managed ISO 13485 submissions and know exactly what it takes to bring a Class II medical device-or a bioactive cosmetic-safely to market.

+2

Currently, I'm expanding my skillset into AI and Machine Learning at U of T, looking for ways to use data to predict material performance.

I'm interested in [Company Name] because you prioritize science-backed claims. I want to bring my ability to design novel biopolymers and my knowledge of global regulatory landscapes to your R&D team, ensuring your next generation of products is both innovative and compliant."

clarify_text.smart_goals:
- Goal 1 (Career Placement): Secure a Senior R&D or Regulatory position at a target pharmaceutical or skincare company (e.g., Johnson & <CANDIDATE_NAME>, Deciem) by August 2026.
- Measurable: Apply to 3 targeted roles weekly; leverage my OBIO/CATTI training to network with 2 industry leaders per month.
- Attainable: My hybrid background makes me a strong candidate for these specific sectors.
- Goal 2 (Technical Expansion): Complete a proof-of-concept project applying CNNs (Machine Learning) to biomaterial image analysis by May 2026.
- Measurable: Submit an abstract on "AI in Biomaterials" to a local conference.
- Realistic: This aligns with my current Postdoc research.
- Goal 3 (Leadership): Transition my current academic leadership roles (GPDSS President) into a professional mentorship role within a "Women in Science" industry network by December 2026.
- Measurable: Join one industry committee or mentorship circle.
- Time-bound: By year-end 2026.

preferences.target_companies:
Pharmaceutical companies (Johnson & Johnson, Eli Lilly, Roche, AbbVie, Sanofi)
Skin care (eg, Deciem, and Crystal Int'l (Group) Inc. & Crystal Claire Cosmetics Inc., Apollo Health and Beauty Care, Cosmetica Laboratories Inc)
